name: Do we need to hire outside specialists to use AEO software effectively, or can our in-house team realistically manage it?

mainEntity.name: Do we need to hire outside specialists to use AEO software effectively, or can our in-house team realistically manage it?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You can absolutely start AEO work in-house, especially if your team already handles SEO, content, or analytics. Most AEO software is built for marketers and strategists, not just engineers, and focuses on interpretable dashboards and practical recommendations.

However, more complex schema implementations and large-scale content restructuring may require some technical or specialist support.

Key factors:
- Existing familiarity with SEO basics and structured data
- Capacity for ongoing content and FAQ updates
- Ability to interpret analytics and visibility reports
- Comfort experimenting and iterating on page templates

Begin with internal ownership: nominate a lead, set up the AEO tool, and run a pilot on a limited page set. Bring in external help only if you hit technical bottlenecks or want faster scale.

Frame AEO as an extension of skills your team already has, with software amplifying their impact.
acceptedAnswer.description: If your in-house team already handles SEO, content, or analytics, they can usually manage AEO software themselves—at least for pilots and early rollout. Reserve outside help for complex schema work or large-scale restructuring; start by letting your team run a focused test and learn the tooling.

description: If your in-house team already handles SEO, content, or analytics, they can usually manage AEO software themselves—at least for pilots and early rollout. Reserve
